expectations.text: Uses Reorder.Group with values and onReorder props
expectations.evidence: TodoList.tsx lines 80-85: `<Reorder.Group axis="y" values={todos} onReorder={setTodos} className="relative flex flex-col gap-2">`

expectations.text: Uses Reorder.Item with key={item.id} and value={item}
expectations.evidence: TodoList.tsx line 89 shows `<TodoRow key={todo.id} todo={todo} ... />` and line 140-141 shows `<Reorder.Item value={todo} dragListener={false} ...>`. The key is on the TodoRow component that renders the Reorder.Item (React key placement pattern), and value={todo} is passed to Reorder.Item.

expectations.text: Uses useDragControls() and dragListener={false} to restrict drag to a handle
expectations.evidence: Line 112: `const dragControls = useDragControls()` and line 142: `dragListener={false}` with `dragControls={dragControls}` on line 143.
Line 191: `onPointerDown={(e) => dragControls.start(e)}` on the drag handle button.

expectations.text: Wraps items in AnimatePresence for enter/exit animations
expectations.evidence: Line 86: `<AnimatePresence mode="popLayout" initial={false}>` wraps the mapped TodoRow components inside Reorder.Group.

expectations.text: Items have initial/animate/exit for smooth add/remove
expectations.evidence: Lines 146-157 on Reorder.Item: `initial={reduce ? { opacity: 0 } : { opacity: 0, y: -12, scale: 0.96 }}`, `animate={reduce ? { opacity: 1 } : { opacity: 1, y: 0, scale: 1 }}`, and `exit={...}` with opacity, x, scale transitions.

expectations.text: For swipe-to-delete, uses drag='x' or onDragEnd with an offset threshold to remove item
expectations.evidence: Line 170: `drag="x"` on the inner motion.div, line 175: `onDragEnd={handleSwipeEnd}`, and lines 129-137 check `info.offset.x < -SWIPE_DELETE_THRESHOLD` (120px) or velocity threshold before calling `onRemove()`.
Lines 2-11: `import { AnimatePresence, Reorder, motion, useDragControls, useMotionValue, useReducedMotion, useTransform } from "motion/react"` and `import type { PanInfo } from "motion/react"`.

expectations.text: Does not use any Motion+ components
expectations.evidence: Only core motion/react APIs used (AnimatePresence, Reorder, motion, useDragControls, useMotionValue, useReducedMotion, useTransform). No Motion+ components like Cursor, Ticker, AnimateNumber, etc. are imported or used.

File extension is .tsx, contains explicit type definitions: `export type Todo = { id: string; text: string; done: boolean }`, `type TodoListProps = { initialTodos?: Todo[] }`, `type TodoRowProps = {...}`, `useState<Todo[]>(initialTodos)`, and typed event handler `(_: PointerEvent, info: PanInfo) => {...}`.

claims.claim: Spring transition values (stiffness 420, damping 32) reflect the skill's house style for physical motion
claims.evidence: Line 158: `transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 420, damping: 32 }}` — response.md explicitly cites this as 'the house style from the skill'.
Line 108 uses `useReducedMotion()` and lines 146-157 conditionally use opacity-only variants when `reduce` is true.

claims.claim: touch-action: none on handle prevents touch scrolling during drag
claims.evidence: Line 194: `style={{ touchAction: "none" }}` on the drag handle button, as stated in response.md.
Line 172: `dragElastic={{ left: 0.9, right: 0 }}` only allows leftward swipe as claimed.

claims.claim: useMotionValue + useTransform animate tint per-frame without re-renders
claims.evidence: Lines 117-127 use useMotionValue and useTransform to drive opacity on the tint element (line 183), which is the correct pattern for non-rerendering animations.

suggestions.assertion: Uses Reorder.Item with key={item.id} and value={item}
suggestions.reason: The assertion literal string `key={item.id}` only matches when Reorder.Item is the direct mapped element. In this solution, the key is placed on the wrapping TodoRow component (a valid and common pattern), not on Reorder.Item itself. A regex-based check would fail this; a structural check (key on the component returned from .map()) would pass. Consider rewording to 'Each item in the list has a stable React key derived from item.id' to avoid penalizing correct implementations that extract the row into its own component.

No assertion verifies that the swipe horizontal drag and the reorder vertical drag don't conflict. This solution correctly uses an inner motion.div for swipe inside Reorder.Item with dragListener={false}, but a naive solution that puts drag='x' directly on Reorder.Item would pass all current assertions while being broken. Consider an assertion like 'swipe and reorder drag targets are separate elements so they do not conflict'.

No assertion covers touch-action handling on the drag handle, which is necessary for the drag handle to work on touch devices. A solution omitting this would pass all assertions but be broken on mobile.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions comprehensively cover the core Motion API usage. Two meaningful gaps: (1) no check that swipe-drag and reorder-drag are on separate elements, (2) no check for touch-action: none on the handle. Also the key={item.id} assertion is phrased in a way that may miss valid patterns where the key lives on a wrapper component.
